questions.text: What tool is primarily used to draw arcs and circles in constructions?
questions.explanation: A compass is used to draw arcs and circles by fixing one point and rotating the pencil end around it.

questions.text: What is a line segment?

options.a: A line extending infinitely in both directions
options.b: A part of a line with two endpoints

questions.explanation: A line segment has two endpoints and includes all points between them.

questions.text: What does a perpendicular bisector do to a line segment?

options.a: Divides it into two unequal parts
options.b: Divides it into two equal parts at a right angle

questions.explanation: A perpendicular bisector divides a line segment into two equal parts at 90 degrees.

questions.text: Which angle is formed by the intersection of two perpendicular lines?
questions.explanation: Two perpendicular lines intersect to form a 90-degree angle.

questions.text: What is the measure of each angle in an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: All angles in an equilateral triangle measure 60 degrees.

questions.text: Which tool is used to measure angles?
questions.explanation: A protractor is used to measure and draw angles.

questions.text: What is the first step to construct a 60-degree angle using a compass and ruler?

options.c: Draw an arc with radius equal to the segment

questions.explanation: First, draw a base line segment to start the construction of the angle.

questions.text: How many sides does a regular hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 equal sides and 6 equal angles.

questions.text: What is the length of all sides in an equilateral triangle?
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all three sides are equal in length.

questions.text: Which figure has all sides equal and all angles 90 degrees?
questions.explanation: A square has all sides equal and all angles 90 degrees.

questions.text: What is the purpose of an angle bisector?

options.b: To divide the angle into two equal parts

questions.explanation: An angle bisector divides an angle into two equal smaller angles.

questions.text: How many degrees are there in a right angle?
questions.explanation: A right angle measures exactly 90 degrees.

questions.text: Which construction tool helps in drawing straight lines?
questions.explanation: A ruler is used to draw straight lines and measure lengths.

questions.text: What is the sum of all interior angles of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of interior angles of any triangle is 180 degrees.

questions.text: To construct a 90-degree angle at a point on a line, what should you construct first?

options.b: A perpendicular line at the point

questions.explanation: Constructing a perpendicular line at the point on the line forms a 90-degree angle.

questions.text: Which of the following shapes can be constructed using only a ruler and compass?
questions.explanation: Square and regular hexagon can be constructed using ruler and compass.

questions.text: If you construct an angle bisector of a 120-degree angle, what is the measure of each smaller angle?
questions.explanation: Bisecting a 120-degree angle divides it into two 60-degree angles.

questions.text: Which step is NOT part of constructing an equilateral triangle using a compass and ruler?

options.b: Draw arcs from both endpoints with the same radius
options.c: Join the intersection point of arcs to endpoints
options.d: Draw a perpendicular bisector of the base

questions.explanation: Drawing a perpendicular bisector is not required to construct an equilateral triangle.

questions.text: How many 60-degree angles are there in a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle of a regular hexagon is 120 degrees, but it can be divided into two 60-degree angles; the hexagon has 6 vertices.

questions.text: Why is a compass important in constructing geometric figures?

options.c: It helps in drawing arcs and circles with fixed radius
options.d: It divides line segments into equal parts

questions.explanation: A compass is used to draw arcs and circles with a fixed radius, essential in geometric constructions.

questions.text: Ravi wants to construct a garden in the shape of an equilateral triangle with each side measuring 12 m. Using a compass and ruler, which angle should he construct at each corner to make the garden perfectly equilateral?
questions.explanation: Each angle in an equilateral triangle measures 60°. To construct it accurately, Ravi must construct 60° angles at each vertex.

questions.text: A carpenter is making a square wooden frame with sides of 30 cm. To ensure the frame is perfectly square, which angle should he construct at each corner using a ruler and compass?
questions.explanation: A square has all four angles equal to 90°. The carpenter needs to construct 90° angles to make the frame square.

questions.text: Meena is drawing a regular hexagon on a poster. If she starts by drawing one side of 10 cm, what angle should she construct at each vertex to complete the hexagon?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle of a regular hexagon is 120°. Meena should construct 120° angles at each vertex.

questions.text: A school is marking a perpendicular bisector on a 24 cm long line segment AB to find its midpoint. What length should the perpendicular bisector be drawn from the midpoint?

options.b: Any length perpendicular to AB at 12 cm

questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector is drawn at the midpoint (12 cm from either end) and can be any length perpendicular to AB at that point.

questions.text: A traffic engineer needs to mark a 90° angle at a road intersection using a compass and ruler. Which construction step is essential to ensure the angle is exactly 90°?

options.b: Constructing a perpendicular bisector of a line segment

questions.explanation: Constructing a perpendicular bisector helps create a 90° angle, essential for right-angled intersections.

questions.text: A craftsman is making a kite with a line segment of 15 cm as one diagonal. To find the exact center, he constructs a perpendicular bisector. At what distance from either end will the bisector intersect the diagonal?
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector intersects the line segment at its midpoint, which is half of 15 cm = 7.5 cm from either end.

questions.text: In a park, a circular flower bed is divided into six equal sections by drawing lines from the center to the edge. What angle should each section have at the center?
questions.explanation: A circle has 360°. Dividing into 6 equal parts means each angle is 360° ÷ 6 = 60°.

questions.text: A student draws a line segment PQ of length 10 cm and wants to construct an angle bisector of a 120° angle at point P. What will be the measure of each angle formed after bisecting?
questions.explanation: Bisecting a 120° angle divides it into two equal angles of 60° each.

questions.text: A carpenter is making a triangular shelf with one angle of 90° and the other two angles equal. Using a compass and ruler, what are the measures of the other two angles?
questions.explanation: In a right-angled triangle with two equal angles, the other two angles are each 45° because 90° + 45° + 45° = 180°.

questions.text: A farmer wants to divide a rectangular field into two equal parts by drawing a line segment and then constructing its perpendicular bisector. Which shape will the two parts form?
questions.explanation: Dividing a rectangle by its perpendicular bisector of one side creates two right-angled triangles of equal area.

questions.text: You are given a line segment AB of length 8 cm. Using only a ruler and compass, describe the steps to construct a square ABCD on AB as one side. After construction, what will be the length of the diagonal AC of the square?

options.a: Steps: Construct perpendicular at A, mark 8 cm for AD, draw parallel to AB through D to get C; Diagonal AC = 8√2 cm
options.b: Steps: Construct perpendicular bisector of AB, mark 8 cm on it for D, join AD and BC; Diagonal AC = 8 cm
options.c: Steps: Construct angle bisector of AB, mark 8 cm for AD, draw parallel to AB through D; Diagonal AC = 16 cm
options.d: Steps: Construct perpendicular at B, mark 8 cm for BC, join AD and DC; Diagonal AC = 4√2 cm

questions.explanation: To construct square ABCD on AB: 1) Draw AB = 8 cm. 2) At point A, construct a perpendicular line using compass and ruler. 3) On this perpendicular, mark point D such that AD = 8 cm. 4) Draw a line through D parallel to AB. 5) Draw a line through B perpendicular to AB to meet the parallel line at C. 6) Join AC. The diagonal AC of a square with side 8 cm is AC = side × √2 = 8√2 cm.

questions.text: Construct an equilateral triangle ABC with side length 6 cm. Then construct the angle bisector of angle BAC. If this bisector meets BC at point D, find the length of BD. (Use properties of equilateral triangle and angle bisector theorem)
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, all sides are equal (6 cm), and all angles are 60°. The angle bisector of angle A also acts as median and altitude, so it divides BC into two equal parts. Hence, BD = DC = BC/2 = 6/2 = 3 cm.

questions.text: You have constructed a regular hexagon by drawing six equilateral triangles around a point O. If each side of the hexagon is 5 cm, calculate the perimeter and the length of the line segment joining two opposite vertices of the hexagon.

options.a: Perimeter = 30 cm; length of segment = 10 cm
options.b: Perimeter = 30 cm; length of segment = 5 cm
options.c: Perimeter = 60 cm; length of segment = 10 cm
options.d: Perimeter = 60 cm; length of segment = 5 cm

questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 equal sides, so perimeter = 6 × 5 = 30 cm. The segment joining two opposite vertices passes through the center and equals twice the side length (since the hexagon can be divided into 6 equilateral triangles), so length = 2 × 5 = 10 cm.

questions.text: Using a compass and ruler, construct a 60° angle at point P. Then construct the perpendicular bisector of the line segment PQ where Q lies on one arm of the 60° angle and PQ = 8 cm. Find the length of the segment from P to the point where the perpendicular bisector meets PQ.
questions.explanation: First, construct 60° angle at P. Mark point Q on one arm such that PQ = 8 cm. The perpendicular bisector of PQ will intersect PQ at its midpoint, which is at 8/2 = 4 cm from P.

questions.text: You have constructed a square ABCD with side 7 cm. Construct the angle bisector of angle ABC. If this bisector intersects diagonal AC at point E, find the length of BE given that triangle ABC is right angled at B.
questions.explanation: In square ABCD, angle ABC = 90°. The angle bisector of 90° divides it into two 45° angles. Using coordinate geometry or properties of square, length BE can be calculated using the angle bisector theorem and Pythagoras theorem, resulting approximately in 4.95 cm.

questions.text: Construct an angle of 120° at point M. On one arm, mark point N such that MN = 10 cm. Construct the perpendicular bisector of MN. Find the distance from M to the point where the perpendicular bisector intersects the other arm of the 120° angle.
questions.explanation: Construct 120° angle at M. Mark N on one arm such that MN=10 cm. The perpendicular bisector of MN is a line perpendicular to MN at its midpoint (5 cm from M). The other arm of the 120° angle forms 120° with MN. Using trigonometry, the distance from M to the intersection point is 5 / sin(60°) = 5 / (√3/2) = 10/√3 ≈ 8.66 cm.

questions.text: Construct a perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB of length 6 cm using ruler and compass. What is the length of each segment after bisecting?
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector divides the segment into two equal parts, each half of 6 cm is 3 cm.

questions.text: Using a compass, construct an angle of 60 degrees at point O. What is the measure of the angle formed?
questions.explanation: An angle of 60 degrees is constructed by drawing an arc and marking equal arcs from the vertex using the compass.

questions.text: You have constructed an equilateral triangle with each side 5 cm. What is the measure of each angle in the triangle?
questions.explanation: All angles in an equilateral triangle are equal and measure 60 degrees.

questions.text: Construct a square ABCD with each side 4 cm. What is the measure of each angle in the square?
questions.explanation: Each angle in a square is a right angle measuring 90 degrees.

questions.text: Using a compass and ruler, construct the bisector of a 120-degree angle. What will be the measure of each angle after bisecting?
questions.explanation: Bisecting a 120-degree angle divides it into two equal angles of 60 degrees each.

questions.text: Draw a regular hexagon with each side 3 cm. How many sides does the hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon always has 6 sides.

questions.text: What is the length of the perpendicular drawn from the vertex of a 90-degree angle to the opposite side when the sides are 5 cm each? (Hint: It is the height of the right angle triangle formed.)
questions.explanation: In a right angle triangle with equal sides of 5 cm, the perpendicular from the vertex to the opposite side is 5 cm.

questions.text: Construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment PQ of length 8 cm. What is the distance from the midpoint to each end?
questions.explanation: The perpendicular bisector divides the segment into two equal parts, each 4 cm long.

questions.text: If you construct an angle bisector of a 90-degree angle, what will be the measure of each resulting angle?
questions.explanation: Bisecting a 90-degree angle divides it into two equal angles of 45 degrees each.

questions.text: Using ruler and compass, construct an equilateral triangle with side 7 cm. What is the perimeter of the triangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter of equilateral triangle = 3 × side = 3 × 7 = 21 cm.

questions.case_text: Ravi is helping his father design a small garden in their backyard in Pune. They want to create a flower bed in the shape of an equilateral triangle with each side measuring 4 meters. Ravi uses a ruler and compass to construct the triangle on a drawing sheet before starting the actual garden work.

subparts.text: Which angle should Ravi construct at each corner of the equilateral triangle?
subparts.explanation: Each angle in an equilateral triangle is always 60 degrees.

subparts.text: To ensure the flower bed is perfectly shaped, Ravi needs to draw the perpendicular bisector of one side. What is the purpose of drawing this perpendicular bisector?

options.a: To divide the side into two equal parts and find the center point
options.b: To measure the length of the side
options.c: To draw an angle of 90 degrees at the corner
options.d: To extend the side to make it longer

subparts.explanation: A perpendicular bisector divides a line segment into two equal parts at 90 degrees, helping find the midpoint.

subparts.text: If Ravi wants to construct the equilateral triangle using a compass, what length should he set on the compass?
subparts.explanation: The compass should be set to the length of the side, which is 4 meters, to draw arcs for the triangle.

questions.case_text: Anjali is designing a decorative tile pattern for her home in Jaipur. She wants to create a regular hexagon tile with each side measuring 6 cm. She plans to use a compass and ruler to draw the hexagon on paper before making the tile.

subparts.text: What is the measure of each interior angle of the regular hexagon Anjali wants to draw?
subparts.explanation: Each interior angle of a regular hexagon is 120 degrees.

subparts.text: To construct the regular hexagon, Anjali first draws a circle with radius 6 cm. What is the significance of this circle in the construction?

options.a: It helps mark the vertices of the hexagon at equal distances on the circumference
options.b: It measures the perimeter of the hexagon
options.c: It divides the hexagon into six equal triangles
options.d: It creates a square inside the hexagon

subparts.explanation: The circle with radius equal to the side length helps locate the six vertices equally spaced on the circumference.

subparts.text: If Anjali wants to draw a 90-degree angle inside her design, which construction tool should she use?
subparts.explanation: A set square is used to construct or measure right angles (90 degrees).
